title: Average (expected) comparison count of randomized quicksort
statement_informal: The average key-comparison count of randomized quicksort, the solution C of n·C(n)=(n+1)·C(n-1)+2(n-1) with C(0)=0, equals 2(n+1)·H_n − 4n where H_n is the n-th harmonic number.

cost_model.notes: Expected key comparisons over a uniformly random permutation. The full-history recurrence reduces to the first-order n·C(n)=(n+1)·C(n-1)+2(n-1); its solution is 2(n+1)H_n−4n (rationals + harmonic numbers).

references: Verify-track twin of the CAS conjecture in tools/conjecture/results/quicksort-average.json (tools/conjecture/cas_explore.py).
Cormen, Leiserson, Rivest, Stein — Introduction to Algorithms, expected running time of randomized quicksort.
Sedgewick & Flajolet — An Introduction to the Analysis of Algorithms.
